title: Configure a Container With Environment Variables
description:
## Pass Configuration at Run Time

Containers are configured from the outside, most often via environment variables.

**Requirements:**
1. Run an **`alpine`** container named **`envtest`** in the background (keep it alive)
2. Set the environment variable **`APP_ENV`** to **`production`** inside it

> 💡 Use `-e KEY=value` on `docker run`. Verify with `docker exec envtest printenv APP_ENV`.

hints.title: Set an env var at run time
hints.body: The -e flag injects an environment variable into the container.
hints.command: docker run -d --name envtest -e APP_ENV=production alpine sleep 3600

hints.title: Read it back
hints.body: printenv inside the container shows the value Docker injected.

validation.description: Checks that APP_ENV=production is set inside the running 'envtest' container.
